negative_variants.description: Zi Nd Discrete Filter: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to Apply zi_nd discrete filter state to the input samples.

Zi Nd Discrete Filter: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use zi_nd() for discrete-time numerator/denominator filtering fail.
Zi Nd Discrete Filter: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Zi Nd Discrete Filter: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for Apply zi_nd discrete filter state to the input samples fails.

Zi Nd Discrete Filter: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use zi_nd() for discrete-time numerator/denominator filtering fail.
